description: Consumer logic assumes a global event order that the broker does not provide across partitions or topics.
body: Failure mode: handlers expect created-before-updated globally, but events for different keys land on different partitions consumed in parallel, so an update can be processed before its create. Detection signals: state corruption when load increases or partitions are added; updates failing because the entity does not exist yet; ordering not enforced by a partition key. Do not assume topic-wide order. Safer fallback: key events for one entity to a single partition, carry a version/sequence, and make handlers tolerant of out-of-order arrival for different keys.
